title: Commentary on Investments: As of December 31, 2026
content:
Portfolio Composition
The investment portfolio is dominated by available-for-sale debt securities as the primary liquidity buffer and income source. Total investments stood at $1,128,520 (in thousands) as of March 31, 2026, a slight increase of 2.0% from the previous period. The portfolio remains weighted toward available-for-sale debt securities, totaling $1,10,212 ($97.6% of total investments). This holding type provides flexibility to sell before maturity if liquidity needs arise.

Other Investments totaled $356 (in thousands), a small share of the portfolio. Time deposits declined by 23.2%, suggesting securities matured or were sold, with proceeds available for other use on the balance sheet.

The investment maturity structure is weighted toward longer maturities (>10 years and >5-10 years). This may reduce near-term liquidity flexibility, since a larger share of the portfolio is not scheduled to mature in the short term.

Taken together, Campus's investment portfolio maintains its role as an income-generating asset base while preserving access to funds through available-for-sale securities. Management continues to monitor the relationship between maturity distribution and immediate cash flow requirements.
